hero.kicker: The API for creators who ship.
hero.headline: One topic. 21 platforms. Zero clicks.
hero.lede: ContentFlow turns a brief into posts, videos, comment replies, and regional distribution flows without making operators babysit every channel.

code_demo.title: Code as product surface, not an afterthought
code_demo.description: Switch between SDKs and watch the same orchestration model drive a live-looking publish queue on the right.

platforms.title: Not just the usual social stack
platforms.description: Generic schedulers flatten every network into the same checkbox list. ContentFlow treats each platform as a real endpoint with its own role in the burst.

features.title: Three systems that make the workflow unfair

bomb.title: Detonate one brief into a full campaign matrix.
bomb.description: Split one idea into platform-native hooks, post bodies, carousel beats, blog outlines, and thumbnail prompts in a single pass.
bomb.bullet: 21 outputs spin up from one input without copy-paste ops.

autopilot.title: Keep every channel responsive without staffing a war room.
autopilot.description: Collect cross-platform replies, classify intent, and draft brand-safe responses before conversations cool off.
autopilot.bullet: Operators stay in control while the queue keeps moving.

viral.title: Rank ideas before production money gets burned.
viral.description: Model packaging quality, hook strength, spread potential, and regional fit so the team only produces what has leverage.
viral.bullet: Greenlight topics with signal, not gut feel.

pricing.title: Buy leverage, not more dashboard debt

free.name: Free
free.description: For testing the API shape and validating your first publishing burst.

build.name: Build
build.description: For solo operators and compact teams who need distribution leverage every week.

scale.name: Scale
scale.description: For brands and agencies running multiple content lanes with real volume targets.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For media ops, internal growth teams, and partners with governance or SLA requirements.

testimonials.quote: We stopped treating cross-platform publishing like a checklist. ContentFlow turned it into one motion system.
testimonials.name: Jiwoo Park

testimonials.quote: The Korea and Japan endpoints mattered. Every generic scheduler looked global until we hit the actual channel mix.

testimonials.quote: Comment Autopilot took the most fragile part of our workflow and made it operational instead of reactive.

faq.title: Questions teams ask before they stop copy-pasting

items.question: Is this just a scheduler with nicer branding?
items.answer: No. The model is orchestration, not calendar management. ContentFlow handles generation, distribution, and reply operations in one system.

items.question: Can we start with one platform and expand later?
items.answer: Yes. Most teams begin with two or three core channels, then expand once the distribution playbook is stable.

items.question: Why highlight Korea and Japan-specific platforms?
items.answer: Because that is where generic global tooling usually breaks. ContentFlow treats regional coverage as a first-class feature, not an afterthought.

items.question: How does video generation connect to publishing?
items.answer: Video jobs can feed directly into a publish workflow so the output URL, metadata, and posting instructions move as one unit.

cta.title: Start with the API. Keep the card in your wallet.
cta.description: Run the first burst on the free tier, then scale once the workflow proves it can carry real volume.

footer.tagline: Signal-first orchestration for creators who need to ship one idea across every relevant channel.
